overview.description: Franklin Lakes is an affluent residential borough in northwestern Bergen County known for its spacious properties, wooded landscapes, and exceptional quality of life. With large-lot zoning, extensive nature preserves, and top-rated schools, it consistently ranks among New Jersey's most desirable communities. The borough maintains a rural, estate-like character while being within commuting distance of New York City.
overview.schoolDistrict: Franklin Lakes Public Schools / Ramapo Indian Hills Regional High School District
highlights: One of New Jersey's wealthiest communities with expansive estate-style homes
Over 1,000 acres of preserved open space and nature areas
Home to the former Becton Dickinson corporate headquarters
Award-winning public school system consistently ranked among NJ's best
Extensive trail systems and reservoirs for outdoor recreation

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Franklin Lakes maintains minimum lot sizes of one to three acres in most zones, preserving the borough's spacious character.
realEstate.recentTrends: Franklin Lakes continues to see strong demand from families seeking top schools and generous lot sizes. Teardowns of older homes and rebuilds with modern luxury homes are common. The market remains competitive with limited inventory.
neighborhoodNotes: Shadow Lake area features waterfront homes along the private lake community
High Mountain section offers elevated properties with panoramic views of the Ramapo Mountains
Franklin Avenue corridor has newer construction and estate homes on 2+ acre lots
Urban Lane / Crystal Lake area provides a more accessible entry point to the borough

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by a contracted private hauler.
details: Trash must be in sealed containers or bags, maximum 50 lbs per bag
Place at curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with the DPW
Yard waste collected separately during spring and fall seasons

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected every other week, following the Bergen County Utilities Authority guidelines.
details: Commingled containers (glass, plastic #1-#7, aluminum, tin) in one bin
Paper, cardboard, and newspaper bundled or in a separate bin
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or hazardous materials
Recycling calendar available on the borough website

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Franklin Lakes is served by a combination of private wells and public water from the Borough's water system. Sewer service is provided through the borough and connected to the Northwest Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Many homes use private wells — annual testing recommended
Public water available in newer developments
Septic systems common in older sections of town
Sewer connections available in designated areas

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Construction Department handles all building permits, inspections, and zoning compliance.
details: Permits required for any structural, electrical, plumbing, or mechanical work
Strict zoning enforcement — lot coverage, setback, and height requirements apply
Tree removal permits required due to environmental ordinances
Applications available at Borough Hall or online

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Franklin Lakes is a suburban community with ample private parking. Limited municipal parking exists at Borough Hall and public facilities.
details: No metered parking in the borough
Parking available at borough parks and recreation facilities
Street parking generally permitted except where signage prohibits
NJ Transit park-and-ride lot available at nearby Oakland station

additionalServices.title: Leaf & Yard Waste Collection
additionalServices.description: Seasonal leaf collection in fall and yard waste pickup in spring and summer.
details: Leaves collected curbside October through December — no need to bag
Branches must be bundled (4 ft max length, 50 lbs max weight)
Grass clippings must be in biodegradable bags
Check the borough calendar for specific collection dates

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers extensive youth and adult programs throughout the year.
details: Youth sports including soccer, basketball, baseball, lacrosse, and tennis
Summer camp programs at borough parks
Adult fitness and wellness programs
Community pool membership available to residents

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Franklin Lakes enforces quiet hours to maintain its residential tranquility.
keyRules: Construction permitted Monday-Friday 7:30 AM to 6:00 PM
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted before 8:00 AM on weekends
Excessive noise complaints handled by the Police Department

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners are required to maintain their lots and structures to preserve neighborhood aesthetics and property values.
keyRules: Lawns must be regularly maintained — excessive growth will result in violation notices
Dead or hazardous trees must be removed (permit required for live tree removal)
Swimming pools must be fenced with self-closing, self-latching gates
No storage of unregistered vehicles or excessive outdoor storage
Snow must be cleared from sidewalks within a reasonable time after snowfall

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: All dogs must be licensed and controlled. The borough has regulations to manage animals given the semi-rural environment.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed annually through the Borough Clerk
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Dogs must be leashed or under voice control when off property
Owners must clean up pet waste from public areas and others' property
Wildlife feeding (deer, bears, etc.) is prohibited

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking regulations focus on safety and preserving the residential character of the community.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets from November 1 to April 1 (snow removal)
Commercial vehicles over a certain weight restricted from parking in residential areas
No parking within 25 feet of intersections or crosswalks
Vehicles must be parked in the direction of traffic flow
Abandoned or unregistered vehicles will be towed

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough standards and state landlord-tenant law.
keyRules: Rental properties must have a Certificate of Occupancy
Landlords must comply with NJ state landlord-tenant regulations
Rental units subject to property maintenance code inspections
Multi-family housing is limited by zoning — most zones are single-family residential
Accessory apartments subject to specific ordinance requirements

other.title: Environmental Protections
other.summary: Franklin Lakes has strong environmental ordinances to protect its natural resources.
keyRules: Tree removal permits required for trees over 8 inches in diameter
Wetlands buffer zones strictly enforced — no construction within designated areas
Stormwater management regulations apply to all new construction
Fertilizer and pesticide restrictions near waterways and wells
Bear-resistant trash container recommendations due to local black bear population

other.title: Deer Management
other.summary: The borough participates in a managed deer reduction program to address overpopulation.
keyRules: Borough-authorized deer management hunts conducted in winter months
Specific designated areas and dates — residents notified in advance
Deer feeding prohibited throughout the borough
Deer-resistant landscaping encouraged

schools.publicDistrict: Franklin Lakes Public Schools (K-8) / Ramapo Indian Hills Regional High School District (9-12)

publicSchools.name: Ramapo High School
publicSchools.notes: Shared regional high school with Wyckoff; strong academics, athletics, and arts programs. Offers numerous AP courses.

publicSchools.name: Franklin Avenue Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Highly rated middle school with advanced programs and extensive extracurriculars

publicSchools.name: High Mountain School
publicSchools.notes: Top-rated elementary school with strong parent involvement

publicSchools.name: Colonial Road School
publicSchools.notes: Neighborhood elementary school known for excellent teachers and community feel

privateSchools.name: Academy of the Most Blessed Sacrament
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving Franklin Lakes and surrounding communities

privateSchools.name: Eastern Christian School
privateSchools.notes: Christian school with campuses in North Haledon serving some Franklin Lakes families; strong academics and faith-based education

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey — Mahwah (6 miles)

whyMoveHere: Top-rated schools with small class sizes and high test scores
Spacious lots (1-3+ acres) offering privacy and a semi-rural feel
Over 1,000 acres of preserved open space, trails, and nature areas
Safe, family-oriented community with low crime rates
Proximity to NYC while maintaining a countryside atmosphere

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus (Route 196) or Drive to train station
commuteToNYC.notes: Most commuters drive to NJ Transit train stations in Oakland or Mahwah (Main/Bergen Line) or take the 196 bus route. Some residents drive to the Radburn station in Fair Lawn for a faster train. The commute is longer than from eastern Bergen County but manageable with express options.

costOfLiving.summary: Franklin Lakes is one of the most expensive communities in Bergen County, with high home prices and property taxes reflecting the quality of life and schools.

pros: Beautiful natural surroundings with extensive preserved open space
Large lots provide privacy and room for families
Very low crime rate — one of the safest communities in NJ
Strong property values and community investment
cons: Long commute to NYC compared to eastern Bergen County towns
Limited public transit options — car-dependent community
Few commercial areas — residents travel to nearby towns for shopping
Bear and deer wildlife encounters require property precautions

restaurants.name: Biagio's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian restaurant in a charming setting, known for fresh pasta, seafood, and an extensive wine list. Popular for special occasions.

restaurants.name: Franklin Steakhouse & Tavern
restaurants.description: Local favorite for classic American steakhouse fare, craft cocktails, and a warm atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Rock & Brew
restaurants.description: Casual gastropub offering craft beers, burgers, and American comfort food with a rock-and-roll theme.

restaurants.name: Urban Farms Market & Kitchen
restaurants.description: Farm-to-table market and prepared foods shop offering fresh, locally sourced meals, salads, and baked goods.

parks.name: Franklin Lakes Nature Preserve
parks.description: A 200+ acre nature preserve with hiking trails through woodlands, wetlands, and along streams. A haven for birdwatching and nature photography.

parks.name: High Mountain Park Preserve
parks.description: A 1,154-acre preserve shared with Wayne and North Haledon, featuring rugged trails to the summit with panoramic views of the NYC skyline, Ramapo Mountains, and surrounding valleys.

parks.name: Municipal Green / Recreation Complex
parks.description: Central recreation area with sports fields, a community pool, playgrounds, and picnic facilities.

parks.name: Shadow Lake
parks.description: A private lake community with swimming, fishing, and boating available to lakefront property owners and members.

events.name: Franklin Lakes Day
events.description: Annual community celebration featuring a parade, live entertainment, food vendors, games, and activities for all ages.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: The borough's annual holiday kickoff with tree lighting ceremony, caroling, hot cocoa, and visits with Santa.

events.name: Summer Concert Series
events.description: Free outdoor concerts at the Municipal Green on select summer evenings, featuring local and regional bands.

events.name: Spring Clean-Up Day
events.description: Community volunteer event to clean parks, trails, and public spaces throughout the borough.

attractions.name: High Mountain Summit
attractions.description: The highest point in the Preakness Range offering sweeping 360-degree views. On clear days, the Manhattan skyline is visible. Popular hiking destination.
attractions.address: High Mountain Park Preserve, Franklin Lakes, NJ 07417

attractions.name: Franklin Lakes Public Library
attractions.description: A well-regarded community library offering extensive collections, children's programming, book clubs, and community events.

attractions.name: Campgaw Mountain Reservation
attractions.description: A Bergen County park just minutes away offering hiking, camping, a ski area, disc golf, and outdoor education — accessible year-round.

shoppingAreas: Franklin Avenue — Small local commercial strip with professional offices and eateries
Wyckoff Shopping Areas — Nearby Wyckoff town center with boutiques and restaurants (5 minutes)
Westfield Garden State Plaza — Major regional mall in Paramus (15 minutes)
Oakland / Wayne commercial corridors — Route 202 shopping and dining options nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Franklin Lakes Borough Hall, 490 DeKorte Drive, Franklin Lakes, NJ 07417

fire.address: Municipal Complex, DeKorte Drive, Franklin Lakes, NJ 07417

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
